intent: task filter-list rule (f3 section 2.3, flagtab.h AUDIT_FILTER_TASK). An -a always,task rule fires at task creation (fork/clone), near-zero net volume when narrowed. Valid, distinct list. Catches a naive 'any -a rule = HIGH' classifier.

rules.note: task-creation list, narrowed by auid. Fires at fork/clone for user tasks; LOW/near-zero net volume. 1200 B/event. Valid distinct list (not 'exit').

suspected_trap: The 'task' filter list (flagtab.h) matches only at fork/clone time and sees inherited task attributes (auditctl(8)), so an always,task rule fires far less and carries near-zero net volume (LOW, ~$0.04/mo here). Two traps: (a) a parser that only knows 'exit'/'filesystem' rejects the valid 'task' list; (b) a classifier that tiers every -a always rule as HIGH over-warns by ~200x. The list must be recognized and the rule tiered LOW.
